title.raw: Marketers are making decisions based on the wrong numbers. That is the problem I am here to solve.
title.rendered: Marketers are making decisions based on the wrong numbers. That is the problem I am here to solve.

content.raw:
> BLOG

Marketers are making decisions based on the wrong numbers. That is the problem I am here to solve.

By Yann Skaalen, Co-founder of Velocity North

Most marketing meetings I have sat in over the last 29 years have one thing in common. Someone opens Google Ads or Meta Ads Manager, points at a number, and a decision gets made. Budgets get moved. Campaigns get paused. Strategies get rewritten. All based on a number that is, in most cases, wrong.

Not slightly wrong. Wrong enough to change the outcome.

That is the problem I have spent my career chasing, and it is the reason I am a partner in Velocity North.

The number on the screen is not the truth

Here is what most marketers do not realise. The conversion numbers shown inside Google Ads and Meta are not measurements. They are estimates produced by the same companies that get paid more when those numbers look better. Add browser tracking prevention, iOS updates, ad blockers, cookie banners, and the GDPR reality of European users, and you end up with a tracking layer that misses anywhere from 30 to 50 percent of actual conversions.

The painful part is not the missing data. The painful part is that the data that does come through is biased in favour of certain campaigns, certain audiences, and certain attribution windows. So when a marketer looks at the dashboard and decides to double down on the campaign with the best ROAS, they are often doubling down on the campaign that the platform happens to track best. Not the campaign that actually drives the most revenue.

In our own customer data we see it constantly. Somewhere between 50 and 70 percent of ad spend goes to campaigns that produce zero attributable revenue once you measure properly. Half the budget. Gone. Every month.

Why I keep coming back to this problem

I have built ad campaigns for 29 years. I have run an agency. I have worked across Norwegian and Swedish markets, with everything from local e-commerce stores to international SaaS companies. And the pattern is always the same. Smart people, good products, real budgets, and a measurement layer that quietly lies to them.

A few years ago I got tired of writing the same disclaimer in every client report. “These numbers are directional. The real picture is probably different.” That is not good enough when someone is spending real money based on what I tell them.

So I founded Digtective. We built a cookieless, GDPR-compliant, server-side tracking and attribution system that gives marketers the actual numbers, not the platform’s interpretation of them. It is now a Shopify-approved app used by e-commerce brands across the Nordics and beyond.

Why Velocity North

Building the technology was the first half of the problem. The second half is making sure it actually changes how marketers make decisions.

That is where Velocity North comes in, and that is why I joined Jakob as a partner.

Jakob and I have built things together before. A few years ago we co-founded a Nordic digital bank, where we ran into the same measurement problem in its hardest possible form. We needed to track loan applications all the way from ad click to approved loan inside the banking system, without breaking GDPR and without leaking personal data to ad platforms. Banks do not get to be sloppy with this. We solved it. That experience is a big part of why I know the measurement problem in marketing is solvable, and why Jakob is the partner I want to solve it with.

Most agencies are good at running campaigns but operate on top of broken data. Most analytics consultancies are good at fixing the data but do not run campaigns. Velocity North sits in the middle. We bring the measurement layer that tells you what is actually working, and we use it to run paid media that compounds instead of plateaus.

In practice my role is the technical and analytical side. Tracking setup, attribution architecture, the unglamorous infrastructure work that determines whether the rest of the strategy is built on sand or on concrete. Jakob brings the strategic and creative side. Together we are the team you bring in when you have outgrown “the dashboard says we are doing fine” and want to know what is actually happening.

Who I want to talk to

If you are a Nordic e-commerce brand or a B2B company spending more than a few thousand euros a month on paid media, and you have a quiet suspicion that your reporting is not telling you the full story, that suspicion is almost certainly correct. I would like to show you what the real numbers look like.

Outside of Velocity North you can find me at Digtective, where we keep building the measurement infrastructure, or at yannsfood.com where I cook a lot of Italian food and write about wine. Both involve obsessing over what actually goes into the result. Same instinct, different output.

Get in touch through the contact page if you want to compare what your platforms are reporting with what is actually happening.

Want to know what your real numbers look like?

Get in touch and let’s compare what the platforms say with what is actually happening.

Get in Touch →

yoast_meta.metadesc: Marketers make decisions on tracking data that misses 30 to 50% of conversions. Yann Skaalen on why he co-founded Velocity North to fix it.
